title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
summary: Zack Proser presents a four-layer architecture for sustainable AI-assisted development: signal agents monitoring Slack and Linear, verification gates from linting to critic passes, weekly agent analysis of conversation history to surface inefficiencies, and integration of health metrics via Oura ring. The approach treats human attention as the bottleneck and uses voice-first workflows and remote control to enable developers to ship code while away from their desk.
writeup: Zack Proser of WorkOS lays out a four-layer architecture for sustainable AI-assisted development that treats human attention as the bottleneck. The layers cover signal agents that monitor Slack and Linear, verification gates ranging from linting to critic passes, weekly agent analysis of conversation history to surface inefficiencies, and integration of health metrics from an Oura ring. The talk pairs this with voice-first workflows and remote control so developers can keep shipping code while away from the desk.
